note: Source URL https://www.iso.org/standard/70791.html is WRONG — that catalogue number does not correspond to ISO 22734. ISO 22734:2019 is at https://www.iso.org/standard/69212.html (confirmed via ISO.org search and ANSI webstore). Fixed to https://www.iso.org/standard/69212.html. Additional note: ISO 22734:2019 has been withdrawn and superseded by ISO 22734-1:2025 (ISO.org standard/82766); the row cites '2019' throughout — consider noting supersession in the summary's [UNVERIFIED] caveat. ISO 14687:2019 similarly superseded by ISO 14687:2025 (ISO.org standard/82660). Standards themselves exist and are correct in substance. Mandatory_status is accurate: NRTL listing is mandatory via OSHA 29 CFR 1910.303 confirmed (osha.gov/laws-regs/regulations/standardnumber/1910/1910.303); ASME U stamp 'mandatory' claim is accurate for most US jurisdictions but not universal — Texas, Louisiana, South Carolina are non-code states that do not require ASME stamping. Phrasing 'most US state and local jurisdictions' is technically correct. Gap description accurate — no mutual recognition between GB/T and ASME/NRTL systems confirmed.

Source URL https://www.energy.gov/eere/fuelcells/hydrogen-shot returns HTTP 404 — page does not exist. Fixed to https://www.energy.gov/articles/clean-hydrogen-production-tax-credit-45v-resources (confirmed live official DOE page explicitly covering 45V resources). T.D. 10026 January 2025 confirmed: Treasury/IRS published final 45V regulations in Federal Register on 2025-01-10 (federalregister.gov/documents/2025/01/10/2024-31513). Official Treasury press release confirmed at home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/jy2768. Mandatory_status correct — 45V is a tax credit incentive, not a product mandate or import bar; Buy America is mandatory only for federally funded projects. Gap description accurate. The row's note that 45V details are subject to potential regulatory revision is appropriate given the 2025 change in US administration; the final rule remains in effect as of the knowledge cutoff.

Source URL https://www.asme.org/codes-standards/find-codes-standards/bpvc-viii-1-bpvc-section-viii-rules-construction-pressure-vessels-division-1 confirmed live and official — ASME product page shows BPVC Section VIII Division 1, 2025 edition, with full scope description. Standard exists and is as described. Mandatory_status claim 'required by most US state boiler and pressure vessel laws' is accurate — confirmed that virtually all states require ASME VIII compliance, with the known exception of non-code states (Texas, Louisiana, South Carolina). Wording 'most US state' is defensible. Gap description (no mutual recognition between TSG/GB 150 and ASME BPVC; 6-18 month timeline) is accurate per industry sources. Note: BPVC current edition is 2025, not 2023; this does not affect substantive compliance requirements described.

Source URL https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-2-standard-development/2 confirmed live — official NFPA 2 Code Development page. NFPA 2:2023 confirmed published and commercially available (ANSI webstore, NFPA store). Note: NFPA 2:2026 has now also been published (nfpa.org/product/nfpa-2-hydrogen-technologies-code/p0002code), but 2023 edition remains in force where adopted by AHJs; 2026 is the current edition. NFPA 55:2023 confirmed live (nfpa.org/product/nfpa-55-code/p0055code). OSHA 29 CFR 1910.103 confirmed at osha.gov/laws-regs/regulations/standardnumber/1910/1910.103 — regulation exists but note its primary scope covers gaseous/liquefied hydrogen delivery systems on consumer premises; its application to electrolyzer installations is via OSHA General Duty Clause and hydrogen classification rules, not a direct electrolyzer-specific mandate. This is a nuance, not an error — the row correctly cites it as an additional federal workplace requirement. Mandatory_status accurate. Gap description accurate — NRTL listing required for electrical equipment in classified locations, no mutual recognition with CCC/CE.
